learning_outcomes.description: Explain the objective of the firm, and apply appropriate approaches to making optimal financial decisions at individual security and portfolio levels.

Apply the time value of money concept to various valuation problems including valuing financial assets (Shares, Bonds, Mortgages) and to make project investment decisions consistent with the firm's objective.
Apply capital budgeting techniques and be able to critically evaluate the resulting analysis in achieving the firm's key objective.
Understand the underlying operation of capital markets, and evaluate the risk and return in a financial context.
Working collaboratively as a group in collecting and analyzing financial data.

assessments.due_date:
Team presentation #1 Week 7 - Week 8

Team presentation #2 Week 12 - Week 12

Peer assessment 28/10/2026 - Week 13

Presentations will take place during class, in your allocated tutorial. Peer Assessment must be completed individually by 5:00 pm on 28 October, the Wednesday of Teaching Week 13.
assessments.task_description:
Students are required to work in teams of 4–5 people (preferably 5), formed with students from their allocated tutorial. Groups will be formed in Teaching Week 1.

Each team will be provided with practical issues to collaboratively analyse and solve outside class time. The assessments will be introduced and explained during lecture time. Every team is expected to deliver two presentations during their allocated tutorial sessions: the first presentation in Teaching Week 6 or 7 and the second presentation in Teaching Week 12. Each team must submit the presentation slides and preparation notes to Blackboard by the end of the relevant presentation week. The topics and the detailed schedule will be published on Blackboard.

Upon completing the team project, you must also complete a Peer Assessment individually by 5 pm on Wednesday of Week 13, 28 October. Peer Assessment will be used to calculate your final grade for the team project. Completing the Peer Assessment is mandatory. If you do not submit a Peer Assessment form, you will receive a score of zero on the team project. Peer Assessment will be completed on Blackboard. Further details will be discussed during the semester. Your presentations will be recorded for marking purposes per UQ Policy.
assessments.submission_guidelines: The presentation slides and preparation notes are submitted using the course Blackboard site. For more details, refer to the "Assessment Guidelines" on Blackboard.
assessments.deferral_or_extension: You may be able to apply for an extension.
assessments.late_submission: A penalty of 10% of the maximum possible mark will be deducted per 24 hours from time submission is due for up to 7 days. After 7 days, you will receive a mark of 0.
assessments.additional_info: You may be able to apply for an extension.

assessments.due_date:
Quiz 1 26/08/2026 5:00 pm

Quiz 2 16/09/2026 5:00 pm

Quiz 3 14/10/2026 5:00 pm

The Blackboard quizzes will be released approximately one week before their respective due dates, and only one attempt will be permitted per quiz. Your best two results out of the three quizzes will count toward your final grade.
assessments.task_description:
This assessment consists of three online mini quizzes delivered via Blackboard tests. Students must complete quizzes individually and independently. Each quiz will be available during a specified time window and may be completed from home, the library, or any other suitable location with stable internet access.

Each quiz will contain a mix of theory-based and calculation-based questions and will assess specific content covered earlier in the course. Only your best two quiz results will count toward your final grade for this assessment.

Further details, including availability periods, quiz coverage, and structure, will be provided on Blackboard in due course.
assessments.deferral_or_extension: You cannot defer or apply for an extension for this assessment.
assessments.late_submission: Exams submitted after the end of the submission time will incur a late penalty.
assessments.additional_info: You cannot defer or apply for an extension for this assessment.

assessments.task_description:
This exam is a closed-book, invigilated, paper-based exam supervised by UQ-appointed invigilators as required by UQ and Professional Accounting organisations.

Your final exam will consist of problem-solving and short-answer questions. For numerical problems, a UQ-approved calculator is permitted during the exam. More details about the final exam will be provided on Blackboard in due course.
assessments.submission_guidelines: You may be able to defer this exam.
assessments.deferral_or_extension: You may be able to defer this exam.
assessments.additional_info: You may be able to defer this exam.
